program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri ve bilgisayar mühendisliğine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimi; bu alanlardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinde kullanabilme becerisine sahip olurlar.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini saptama, tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi; bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisine sahip olurlar.

Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisi; bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama becerisine sahip olurlar.

Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları geliştirme, seçme ve kullanma becerisi; bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisine sahip olurlar.

Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya bilgisayar mühendisliği araştırma konularının incelenmesi için deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisine sahip olurlar.
Disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi; bireysel çalışma becerisine sahip olurlar.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği bilinci; bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisine sahip olurlar.

Türkçe sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi; yabancı bir dilde etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisine sahip olurlar.
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk bilinci; mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi sahibi olurlar.

Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgi; girişimcilik, yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık; sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi sahibi olurlar.

Mühendislik uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgi; mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalıkları vardır.
Türevsel denklemler, integral hesapları, doğrusal cebir, istatistik ve olasılık, ve ayrık matematik içerecek şekilde ileri matematik konularında bilgi sahibi olurlar.

Karmaşık elektrik ve elektronik cihazların, yazılımların ve donanım ve yazılım içeren sistemlerin tasarım ve analizi için gerekli matematik bilgisi, temel bilimler, bilgisayar bilimleri ve mühendislik bilimleri konularında bilgi sahibi olurlar.

Bilgisayar mühendisliğinde veri yapıları ve algoritma analizi, veri tabanı yönetim sistemleri, işletim sistemleri, yazılım mühendisliği, bilgisayar mimarisi ve otomat kuramı alanlarında bilgi sahibi olurlar.

courses.name: Physics I
courses.purpose: The course aims to enable students to analyze force systems related to dynamics of motion, solve problems involving displacement, velocity, and acceleration under applied forces, explain Newton's laws of motion and apply them to uniformly accelerated motion, conduct controlled experiments on linear, circular, and simple harmonic motion, and calculate work and relate it to energy, including the conservation of energy, work, and momentum.

learning_outcomes: Demonstrates the ability to use appropriate concepts to qualitatively analyze situations or problems involving physics.
Demonstrates the ability to use appropriate mathematical techniques and concepts to obtain quantitative solutions in physics problems.
Learns the fundamental principles governing the physical universe.
Understands the fundamental principles of physics and the concepts of motion, force, work, energy, and momentum.
Demonstrates an understanding of physical and environmental reality and the underlying fundamental physical principles behind a wide variety of natural phenomena and their interconnections.

courses.name: Discrete Computational Structures
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the discrete computational structures that are very important for computer science. By the end of the course, students are expected to be able to prove hypotheses using proof methods, understand number theory concepts and counting techniques, solve recurrence relations, and identify problems related to graph theory and trees.
courses.description: The course starts with logic and sets and continues with functions. Then proof methods, including induction, are covered in detail. Recurrence relations, number theory, counting techniques, graph theory, and related emerging problems and known related algorithms are other topics covered.
learning_outcomes: Solve logic problems using algebraic rules.
Prove hypotheses using proof methods.
Express number theory concepts and counting techniques.
Solve recurrence relations.
Identify problems related to graph theory and trees and apply related algorithms.
Express relations and their properties.

resources: Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications 7th edition, Kenneth H. Rosen, McGraw Hill

courses.name: Linear Algebra for Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı öğrencilerin lineer denklem sistemlerini çözebilmesini, kare matrislerin determinantını ve tersini bulabilmesini, vektör uzaylarıyla ilgili temel kavramları ifade edebilmesini, eigen değer ve eigen vektörleri hesaplayabilmesini ve ortogonal vektörleri tespit edebilmesini sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Ders lineer denklem sistemleri ve bunların çözüm yöntemleriyle başlar. Ardından vektör denklemleri, matris cebiri, faktörizasyon ve determinantlar işlenir. Devamında vektör uzaylarıyla ilgili kavramlar, eigen vektör ve eigen değerler, köşegenleştirme ve son olarak ortogonallik ele alınır.
learning_outcomes: Bir lineer denklemler kümesini matris ve vektör cebirini kullanarak çözebilmek
Bir kare matrisin determinantını ve varsa tersini hesaplayabilmek
Vektör uzayları ile ilgili lineer bağımlı kümeleri, taban gibi temel kavramları ifade edebilmek
Bir kare matrisin eigen değerlerini ve eigen vektörlerini bulabilmek ve köşegenleştirmeyi yapabilmek
Ortogonal vektörleri tespit edebilmek
resources: Linear Algebra and Its Applications, David C. Lay, 4th Ed., Pearson

courses.name: Data Structures
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide knowledge about data structures, analyze some data structures, and help students choose the most appropriate data structure in programming and learn their varieties.
courses.description: Data types, algorithmic notation, format conventions, statements and control structures, introduction to algorithm analysis in terms of time and space requirements, basic data structures, strings, recursion, linear data structures and their sequential representations in memory, arrays, stacks and queues, lists, singly, circular and doubly linked lists, non-linear data structures, trees, binary trees and their representations, multi-linked structures, graph structures and their representations, dynamic memory management, hashing techniques.
learning_outcomes: Create complex data types
Abstract physical data
Express basic data structures
Analyze algorithms asymptotically in terms of running time and memory space
Explain static and dynamic data structures
Design and implement data structures such as stacks, queues, basic and special trees, hashing, heaps or priority queues, and graphs
Understand and apply quadratic and O(nlogn) in-memory sorting algorithms
Understand and implement node search, insertion, deletion, and update operations on data structures and analyze their time/space complexities
Create efficient data structures that exactly represent the input-output relationship of a given problem and develop the related operations on these structures
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and iteration (Objectives, review of mathematical tools, and static and dynamic data structures)
Hafta 3: Recursion and recursive equation solutions
Hafta 6: Basic tree structures (General trees, binary and d-ary trees, binary search trees)
Hafta 7: Special tree structures (AVL, splay and B-trees)
Hafta 12: Sorting methods (Selection sort, bubble sort and insertion sort)
Hafta 13: Sorting methods (Mergesort, Quicksort and Heapsort)
Hafta 14: Graph algorithms (Graphs, Topological Sort, breadth-first and depth-first search)
Hafta 15: Graph algorithms (Single-source shortest path problem, Minimum Spanning Trees)
resources: Mark Allen Weiss, Data Structures & Algorithms Analysis in C 2nd edition, Addison-Wesley Publishing Company, 1999
Thomas H. Cormen, Charles E. Leiserson, Ronald L. Livest and Clifford Stein, Introduction to Algorithms, 2nd edition, MIT Press, 2003

courses.name: Engineering Economy
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the economic aspects of evaluating and selecting alternative investment projects. By the end of the course, students are expected to gain the ability to solve advanced engineering economy problems within the broader context of engineering economy feasibility.
courses.description: Introduction to economics, detailed research on economic policies in the world, central banks, money markets, and stock markets.
learning_outcomes: Have knowledge about engineering economy.
Have knowledge of cash flow and management.
Have knowledge about price-cost and supply-demand balance.
Know investment alternatives and compare them, and apply them to real investment problems in order to make the final investment decision.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Market interest rates and debt management
Hafta 10: Depreciation and income tax accounting
Hafta 12: Uncertainty in projects and risk management
resources: Engineering Economy, William G. Sullivan, Elin M. Wicks, James T. Luxhoj, Prentice Hall Company. Twelfth edition. 2003

courses.name: Electric Circuits
courses.purpose: To enable students to learn the models of basic electric circuits and circuit elements. To develop the ability to analyze basic electric circuits and to apply mathematics, basic sciences, and engineering knowledge for analysis and modeling purposes.
courses.description: Definition and modeling of charge, current, power, and energy functions. Ideal circuit elements. Independent and dependent voltage and current sources. Kirchhoff's current and voltage laws. Analysis of simple resistive circuits. Mesh current and node voltage methods. Thevenin and Norton equivalent circuits. Analysis of circuits containing operational amplifiers and resistors. Capacitor and inductor circuit elements. Analysis of first-order circuits containing capacitors or inductors. Sinusoidal steady-state analysis. Phasor concept. Transformation of circuits with sinusoidal sources into the frequency domain and application of basic circuit analysis methods in the frequency domain.
learning_outcomes: Explain and use basic electrical concepts such as current, voltage, power, and energy, as well as basic circuit elements.
Apply basic circuit analysis and solution methods (Kirchhoff's laws, node-voltage, mesh-current methods, etc.) to circuits containing dependent and independent sources.
Analyze and/or design circuits containing operational amplifiers and basic circuit elements using analytical methods.
Analyze first-order circuits containing capacitors or inductors using analytical methods.
Transform circuits with sinusoidal sources into the frequency domain and apply basic circuit analysis methods in the frequency domain.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to circuit theory and main topics
Hafta 2: Analysis of circuits using Kirchhoff and Ohm laws
Hafta 4: Delta and wye transformations, mesh-current method
Hafta 6: Ideal OPAMP combined with resistive circuits
Hafta 7: Source transformations, superposition principle, Thevenin and Norton equivalent circuits
Hafta 10: Natural and step responses of first-order RL and RC circuits
Hafta 11: Periodic signals, RMS value, circuit analysis in the frequency domain
resources: James W. Nilsson, Susan A. Riedel: “Electric Circuits”, 8th Ed., Prentice Hall, New Jersey, 2008.

courses.name: Numerical Methods
courses.purpose: To provide mathematical and computational methods used to solve engineering equations.
courses.description: Taylor polynomials, error and computer arithmetic, root finding, interpolation and estimation, numerical differentiation and integration, solution of systems of linear equations, curve fitting, and numerical solution of differential equations.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to determine the roots of systems of equations using various numerical methods.
Students will be able to fit a curve to functions using linear regression and polynomial regression, and calculate the coefficient of determination for the fit.
Students will be able to estimate an intermediate value on a function through interpolation.
Students will be able to perform differentiation and integration using numerical methods.
Students will be able to solve ordinary differential equations using the Euler method and Runge-Kutta methods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 14: Numerical solution of differential equations
Hafta 15: Numerical solution of differential equations
resources: Atkinson, K. E., Han, W., Elementary Numerical Analysis, 3rd Edition, John Wiley & Sons, Inc.
Numerical Methods for Engineers, 4th Ed., Steven C. Chapra and Raymond P. Canale, McGraw-Hill, 2002.
Numerical Analysis, 3rd Ed., Melvin C. Maron and Robert J. Lopez, Wadsworth, Inc., 1991.

courses.name: Systems Programming
courses.purpose: To help students understand the machine code generated by the C compiler on the x86-64 architecture and to enable them to write high-performance C code.
courses.description: Integer and floating point data types in C and arithmetic and logical operations on them. Instructions in the Intel x86-64 instruction set architecture. Methods for writing high-performance code in C. Memory hierarchy. Cache types and the impact of cache on program performance. Dynamic memory allocation methods.
learning_outcomes: Explain how integer and floating point data types are stored in memory.
Understand and explain the x86-64 assembly language generated by the C compiler.
Write high-performance C code.
Explain the storage units in the memory hierarchy and how these units are organized.
Understand and explain how dynamic memory management is performed in C.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Integer Veri Tipi ve İşlemler

Hafta 2: Integer Veri Tipi ve İşlemler Temel tanımlar ve bilgisayar ortamında veri gösterimi/saklanması Temel tanımlar ve bilgisayar ortamında veri gösterimi/saklanması Temel tanımlar ve bilgisayar ortamında veri gösterimi/saklanması Temel tanımlar ve bilgisayar ortamında veri gösterimi/saklanması
resources: Randal E. Bryant and David R. O'Hallaron, Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective, Third Edition, Pearson, 2016

courses.name: Analysis of Algorithms
courses.purpose: To inform students about runtime analysis techniques. To clearly and understandably present methods for designing correct and efficient algorithms. To provide information on effectively using appropriate data structures while developing algorithms.
courses.description: Introduction: basic problem-solving methods, important problem types, basic data structures. Fundamentals of time and space efficiency analysis of algorithms. Mathematical efficiency analysis of iterative and non-iterative algorithms. Brute force method. Reduce-and-conquer method. Divide-and-conquer method. Transform-and-conquer method. Methods that use more memory to save time. Dynamic programming. Greedy algorithms. Iterative improvement algorithms. Limits in algorithm efficiency. P, NP, NP-Complete problems. Methods for solving hard problems.
learning_outcomes: To be able to mathematically analyze the time and space complexity of algorithms.
To be able to experimentally compare the performance of different algorithms.
To be able to apply various algorithm design techniques to different problems.
To be able to analyze lower bounds of problems and determine complexity classes.
To be able to design efficient algorithms for hard problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: algorithm concept, fundamentals of algorithmic problem solving, important problem types, main data structures
Hafta 2: Fundamentals of algorithm efficiency analysis, asymptotic notations
Hafta 3: Mathematical analysis of iterative and non-iterative algorithms
Hafta 4: Brute force and exhaustive search methods
Hafta 9: Algorithms that use more memory to save time
Hafta 13: Lower bound arguments. Decision trees.
Hafta 15: Methods for solving hard problems
resources: A. Levitin. Int to the Design and Analysis Algorithms. Addison-Wesley.
J. Kleinberg and E. Tardos, Algorithm Design, Addison-Wesley, 2005.
T.H. Cormen, C.E. Leiserson, R. Rivest, and C. Stein, Algorithms (2nd ed.), MIT Press, 2001.

courses.name: Principles of Programming Languages
courses.purpose: To introduce students to the fundamental concepts that support programming languages and to develop their ability to think about program behavior. It also aims to enable students to develop projects using different programming languages.
courses.description: Syntax and semantics definition, lexical and syntactic analysis, names, bindings, type checking, variables, data types, expressions, assignment statements, statement-level control structures, subprograms, subprogram implementation, parameter passing, dynamic and static memory management, abstract data types, logic programming, and functional programming.
learning_outcomes: To be able to define a given grammar and write a parser for that language.
To gain knowledge about logic and functional languages and be able to develop programs with these languages.
To be able to comment on the scope and lifetime of variables in dynamic/static programming languages.
To be able to comment on the working principles of logical and mathematical expressions in different types of programming languages.
To be able to comment on the working principles of different parameter passing methods for subprograms.
resources: Robert W. Sebesta, Concepts of Programming Languages, 11th edition, Pearson, 2016

courses.name: Differential Equations
courses.purpose: This course aims to present the basic theory of ordinary differential equations and provide students with the tools needed to solve equations modeled by differential equations and related application problems.
courses.description: Definition and classification of differential equations; first-order differential equations; second-order differential equations; Laplace transform; systems of linear differential equations; series solutions of linear differential equations.
learning_outcomes: Solve first-order differential equations using techniques such as direct integration, separation of variables, homogeneous, exact and linear equations, and Laplace transform methods.

Solve second- and higher-order differential equations using reduction of order; homogeneous equations with constant coefficients; methods of undetermined coefficients and variation of parameters; series solutions; and Laplace transform methods.
Solve systems of first- and higher-order linear differential equations using operator methods, matrix methods, and Laplace transform methods.
Find particular solutions of ordinary differential equations for given boundary conditions or initial conditions.
Develop mathematical models for some real-life engineering systems and apply appropriate techniques to solve ordinary differential equations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition and classification of differential equations. Initial-value and boundary-value problems.
Hafta 2: First-order differential equations - exact differential equations. Integrating factors.
Hafta 3: First-order differential equations - separable and homogeneous equations.
Hafta 4: First-order differential equations - linear and Bernoulli equations.
Hafta 5: Applications of first-order differential equations.
Hafta 6: Second- and higher-order differential equations - homogeneous linear equations.
Hafta 7: Second- and higher-order differential equations - homogeneous equations. Method of undetermined coefficients.
Hafta 9: Second- and higher-order differential equations - variation of parameters. Cauchy-Euler equation.
Hafta 10: Laplace transform. Inverse transform. Laplace transform. Inverse transform. Convolution.
Hafta 11: Laplace transform. Solution of linear differential equations.
Hafta 12: Systems of linear differential equations. Differential operators.
Hafta 13: Linear differential equation systems in normal form. Matrices and vectors.
Hafta 14: Series solutions of linear differential equations. Power series solutions.
Hafta 15: Series solutions of linear differential equations. Frobenius method.
resources: Introduction to Ordinary Differential Equations (4th Edition), by S.L. Ross, Wiley 1989.
Differential Equations, by S.L. Ross, Wiley 1984.
Elementary Differential Equations and Boundary Value Theorems, by W. Boyce and R. C. DiPrima, Wiley 2008.

courses.name: Electronics
courses.purpose: To develop a fundamental understanding of the theory and application of electronic circuits, and to enable students to analyze and design electronic circuits.
courses.description: Semiconductor materials, diode circuit analysis, introduction to transistor circuits, bipolar junction transistor (BJT) and field effect transistor (FET).
learning_outcomes: Understands the structure and operation of nonlinear circuits.
Understands semiconductor materials and components.
Will be able to apply basic circuit analysis concepts and nonlinear circuits including diodes.
Learns different transistor types.
Will be able to analyze basic electronic circuits.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Semiconductor diode structure and operating principle
Hafta 7: BJT DC analysis and examples
Hafta 10: BJT AC analysis and examples
Hafta 11: MOSFET structure and operating principle
Hafta 13: MOSFET DC analysis and examples
Hafta 15: MOSFET AC analysis and examples
resources: R. Boylestad and L. Nashelsky, Electronic Devices and Circuit Theory, 10th Ed., Prentice Hall, 2009.

courses.name: Probability and Statistics for Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: To introduce the fundamental concepts of probability and statistics. To teach probability models and statistical methods needed in advanced courses, and to enable students to analyze data and perform hypothesis tests on data.
courses.description: Introduction to probability, basic definitions, axioms related to probability and combinatorial concepts; describing data with graphs; describing data with numerical measures and describing bivariate data; conditional probability and independence; probability and probability distributions; discrete random variables and probability distributions; continuous random variables and probability distributions; normal probability distribution; jointly distributed random variables; expected value, covariance and correlation; sampling distributions; large-sample estimation; large-sample hypothesis testing; estimation from small samples.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic concepts of probability theory and continuous and discrete distributions.
Calculate the probabilities of events involving one or more random variables.
Represent the distribution of a data set graphically.
Analyze statistical data.
Perform hypothesis tests on data using statistical methods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to probability, basic definitions, axioms related to probability and combinatorial concepts
Hafta 2: Describing data with graphs; describing data with numerical measures, describing bivariate data
Hafta 5: Discrete random variables and probability distributions
Hafta 6: Continuous random variables and probability distributions
Hafta 10: Expected value, covariance and correlation
resources: Mendenhall, Beaver, Beaver, Introduction to Probability and Statistics, 14th Ed., Brooks/Cole, Cengage Learning, 2013
Walpole, Myers, Myers, Ye, Essentials of Probability and Statistics for Engineers and Scientists, Pearson Education, 2013
Sheldon Ross, A First Course in Probability, 9th Ed., Pearson, 2013/2014

courses.name: Summer Practice I
courses.purpose: To gain practical and technological experience in managing and carrying out real-world problems in industrial engineering and information organizations through individual and team work. To develop the ability to effectively turn the stages of implementing a defined problem into a report.
courses.description: Observation and gaining knowledge about the processes of managing/carrying out problems in industrial engineering and information organizations. Gaining knowledge about a technical real-world problem to be solved and performing the related software requirements analysis, design, software development, and testing. Reporting the implemented system and the experiences gained.
learning_outcomes: Practical and technological experience in managing/carrying out real-world problems in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Experience in developing a system or application in industrial engineering and information organizations through individual and team work.
Ability to perform software requirements analysis for a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations effectively, individually and within a team.
Ability to perform software design for a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations effectively, individually and within a team.
Ability to develop and test the software of a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations effectively, individually and within a team.
Ability to effectively transform the implementation stages of a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations into a report.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Observation and gaining knowledge about the processes of managing/carrying out problems in industrial engineering and information organizations.

Hafta 2: Gaining knowledge about a technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations, receiving the necessary training. Performing software requirements analysis and design for the technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Hafta 3: Performing software requirements analysis and design for the technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Hafta 4: Performing the software development and testing of the technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Hafta 5: Transforming the implemented system into a report.
resources: Before Summer Practice: 1. Review the summer practice rules on the website. 2. Attend the summer practice seminar and ask questions. 3. Inform the internship and student office about your summer practice start date 3 weeks before the actual start date.

courses.name: Database Systems
courses.purpose: The course introduces students to the fundamental concepts of database design and teaches database theory through theoretical hours and a popular DBMS software through practice hours. Students are also expected to develop a real database system term project.
courses.description: Introduction; database environment and development process; data modeling in an organization; E-R model; advanced E-R model; logical database design and relational model; introduction to SQL; SQL statements; advanced SQL; physical database implementation; functional dependency and normalization; physical database design and performance; indexes; stored procedures and triggers; database application development; data and database management; data warehouse.
learning_outcomes: Write SQL code for data definition, constraints, schema, queries, and transactions.
Model data with the entity-relationship (ER) model and perform relational database design.
Understand database concepts, applications, data models, schemas, and objects.
Understand constraints and relational algebra operations.
Create, design, and improve databases using database management system software.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction; database environment and development process
Hafta 4: Logical database design and relational model
Hafta 11: Physical database design and performance; indexes
resources: Modern Database Management; 12th Edition; J.A. Hoffer, R. Venkataraman, H. Topi; Pearson; 2016.
Fundamentals of Database Systems; 7th Edition; R. Elmasri, S.B. Navathe; Pearson; 2016.
Fundamentals of Database Systems; 6th Edition; R. Elmasri, S.B. Navathe; Addison Wesley; 2011.

courses.name: Object-Oriented Software Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental concepts and philosophy of object-oriented software design, and to provide detailed knowledge about the notation and processes used in the software industry. It also aims to help students apply this knowledge to problems in different domains.
courses.description: Software complexity and the structure of complex systems; classes and objects; notation and UML; encapsulation and composition; inheritance and interfaces; MVC architecture; design patterns.
learning_outcomes: Explain the distinction between object-oriented and procedural programming approaches and the advantages of the object-oriented approach.
Write a requirements analysis document supported by class diagrams and system sequence diagrams, individually and in teams.
Create UML class and sequence diagrams for software design, individually and in team work.

Understand software design tools such as requirements analysis documents and UML class and sequence diagrams, and effectively transform them into code individually and in teams; explain popular software design patterns.
Learn the basic features of the Python programming language and gain project implementation skills in this language.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General introduction to the course.
Hafta 2: Software complexity and the structure of complex systems.
Hafta 5: Domain class and system sequence diagrams for software requirements analysis.
Hafta 6: UML class and object diagrams for software design.
Hafta 7: UML sequence diagrams for software design.
Hafta 9: Solution session for midterm questions.
Hafta 11: General features of the Python programming language and differences from C++/Java programming languages.
Hafta 12: Object-oriented programming in Python, classes, encapsulation, inheritance.
resources: Object-Oriented Analysis and Design with Applications (Third Edition) by Grady Booch ISBN 0-201-89551-X
UML Distilled (Third Edition) by Martin Fowler ISBN 0-321-19368-7
Design Patterns (Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software) by Erich Gamma ISBN 0-201-63361-2

courses.name: Digital Logic Design
courses.purpose: This course introduces the principles of logic circuit design. It aims to enable students to efficiently analyze and design combinational and sequential circuits.
courses.description: Digital systems. Boolean algebra. Combinational logic design. Sequential logic design. Logic design optimization. FSM and HLSM. Design of datapath components. Counter-transfer level design.
learning_outcomes: Use theorems and properties of Boolean algebra to manipulate logical expressions and simplify them.
Design and solve combinational circuits.
Realize logical functions using Karnaugh maps, including functions with unspecified inputs.
Design and solve sequential circuits.
Perform digital logic design at RTL level using the Verilog hardware description language.
Design datapath components using Verilog and Logism under specific constraints.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: General properties of digital systems
Hafta 5: Combinational Logic Design - Improvements and Trade-offs
resources: Nelson P. V., Agle H.T., Carroll D B., Irwin J. D., 1995; Digital Logic Circuit Analysis and Design, Prentice Hall, New Jersey

courses.name: Modeling and Discrete Simulation
courses.purpose: To provide experience in applying simulation principles to studied systems, alongside the methods used in discrete system simulation, in order to design and analyze systems effectively.
courses.description: Modeling and simulation; general principles and concepts of discrete-event simulations; mathematical and statistical modeling in simulation; queue modeling; random number generation; generation of various distributions; simulation data analysis, input data modeling, verification and validation of simulation models; output analysis; evaluation of alternative system designs.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic principles related to simulation modeling and the analysis of discrete-event systems.
Perform input analysis to determine the random distributions and their parameters to be used in a simulation model.
Apply methods of random number and random variable generation to obtain inputs that represent uncertainties in a system.
Perform output analysis of simulations and estimate system performance.
Design an alternative system for a system and compare its performance using statistical methods.
Build, run, and report the outputs of a model for a specific system using a simulation software package.
weekly_topics: Hafta 12: Evaluation of alternative system design
resources: Banks, Carson, Nelson, Nicol, Discrete-Event System Simulation, 5th Edition, Pearson 2010.
Lawrence M. Leemis and Stephen K. Park, Discrete-Event Simulation: A First Course, Prentice Hall, 2006.
Michael Pidd, Computer Simulation in Management Science, 5th Edition, Wiley, 2004.

courses.name: Operating Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the fundamental concepts underlying modern operating systems. It also seeks to provide hands-on experience through programming projects that support these concepts.
courses.description: Fundamental topics and components of operating systems. System functions. Process management and interprocess communication techniques. Threads and application design using a thread library. CPU scheduling algorithms. Process synchronization, synchronization primitives, and classical synchronization problems. Deadlocks, deadlock prevention and avoidance techniques. Memory management techniques and paging. Virtual memory management. File management and file systems. Basics of Linux programming.
learning_outcomes: To be able to define the fundamental topics, purposes, components, and functions of modern operating systems.
To be able to explain and compare various approaches proposed for the fundamental problems of operating systems.
To be able to explain and analyze various algorithms used in operating systems.
To understand the basics of the open-source Linux operating system, use it, develop programs on it, and perform scripting programming.
To be able to write multithreaded applications that can solve a synchronization problem.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Operating Systems and General Overview, Interrupts, CPU Operating Modes
Hafta 2: Introduction to Operating Systems and General Overview, Interrupts, CPU Operating Modes
Hafta 3: Operating System Structures, System Functions, Examples of System Functions
Hafta 4: Processes and Process Management, Linux Processes
Hafta 5: Processes and Process Management, Linux Processes
Hafta 6: Process Virtual Address Spaces and the Address Space of a Program Written in C
Hafta 8: Process and Thread Scheduling Algorithms
Hafta 9: Process Synchronization, Synchronization Tools, and Classical Synchronization Problems
Hafta 10: Process Synchronization, Synchronization Tools, and Classical Synchronization Problems
Hafta 11: Resource Deadlocks, Deadlock Prevention and Avoidance Techniques
Hafta 12: Resource Deadlocks, Deadlock Prevention and Avoidance Techniques
Hafta 14: Virtual Memory Management and File Systems
resources: Operating System Concepts, 10th edition, Silberschatz, Galvin, Gagne, John Wiley, 2021.
Operating Systems: Internals and Design Principles, 5th edition, W. Stallings, Prentice Hall.
Practical Unix Programming, K. Robbins and S. Robins, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Computer Organization
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamentals and design details of modern computer architecture and organization. It aims to enable students to analyze and design datapath components and control units in a simple computer architecture efficiently.
courses.description: Performance analysis in different computer architectures. Instruction Set Architecture. MIPS instructions. Arithmetic operations. Design of datapath components. Datapath and control design for single-cycle and pipeline-based architectures. Memory hierarchy.
learning_outcomes: Measure the performance of a computer system and compare different computer systems using performance parameters and metrics related to computer architecture.
Understand the fundamentals of MIPS Instruction Set Architecture and be able to understand, design, and develop programs in MIPS assembly language.
Explain the steps of arithmetic operations for integer and fractional numbers and how the related hardware works.
Design a basic single-cycle datapath and its control units for a given instruction set, and extend it with new instructions.
Understand pipeline datapaths and related control units, analyze hazards, and propose solutions to hazards.
Design and analyze simple cache systems using the basic principles of memory hierarchy.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: CPU performance measurement and evaluation; CPU benchmarks
Hafta 3: Instruction Set Architecture; Introduction to MIPS instructions
Hafta 4: MIPS instructions; addressing modes; sample MIPS programs
Hafta 5: Arithmetic operations; multiplication and division operations and required hardware
Hafta 6: Representation of fractional numbers; arithmetic operations for fractional numbers
Hafta 10: Datapath design for single-cycle architecture
Hafta 11: Control design for single-cycle architecture
Hafta 13: Datapath and control design for pipeline-based architecture
resources: Computer Organization and Design: The Hardware/Software Interface, 5th edition, by David A. Patterson and John L. Hennessy, Morgan Kaufmann Publishers, 2020

courses.name: Software Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce third-year computer engineering students to the stages of the software process and to make them practice the fundamental methods used in those stages. It also helps students recognize the challenges encountered in software development and develop solutions to them.
courses.description: Software processes, project management, software requirements, requirements engineering, software architectural design, distributed software design, object-oriented design, real-time software design, rapid software development processes, verification and validation subprocesses, and software cost estimation.
learning_outcomes: Understand the software process and its stages
Define basic software process models
Express hybrid and more complex software process models
Compare and select process models according to the type of a sample software problem
Apply the tools used in the requirements engineering stage to this subprocess
Use the tools in the 5th learning outcome to define requirements in a sample problem
Understand the creative nature of the software design stage, classify design approaches, and use the correct approaches for suitable problems
Discuss design approach options and determine and apply a suitable approach for a sample problem
Comprehend the verification and validation stage, its spread throughout the whole process, and its deep effect on software quality
Prepare the necessary test plans that satisfy the requirements in outcome 6
Perform tests
Understand software evolution as the only stage that starts after delivery to the customer and as the stage that enables software to adapt to changing requirements over time
Prepare a report covering all stages of the process related to the development of a sample software problem
weekly_topics: Hafta 15: Yazılım niteliği ve süreç iyileştirme
Hafta 16: Dönem sonu sınavı hazırlık çalışması
resources: Software Engineering, Ian Sommerville, 8th edition, Addison-Wesley, 2007
The Unified Software Development Process, Ivar Jacobson, Grady Booch and James Rumbaugh, Addison Wesley, 1999

courses.name: Formal Languages and Automata Theory
courses.purpose: This course provides students with an overview of the theoretical foundations of computer science and introduces the fundamental concepts and models of formal languages and automata theory. It also aims to develop students' abstract thinking and proof-writing abilities.
courses.description: Deterministic finite automata. Nondeterministic finite automata. Regular expressions and regular languages. Properties of regular languages. Context-free grammars and properties of context-free languages. Pushdown automata. Turing machines. Undecidability.
learning_outcomes: Be able to identify regular languages, understand their properties, and design deterministic finite automata.
Be able to design nondeterministic finite automata and identify regular expressions.
Be able to identify context-free languages and design grammars or pushdown automata for these languages.
Be able to understand and design the operation of a Turing machine and identify decidable/undecidable languages/problems.
Be able to perform formal proofs within the scope of automata theory.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Automata Theory and the Concept of Language
Hafta 2: Deterministic Finite Automata and Regular Languages
Hafta 3: Deterministic Finite Automata and Regular Languages
Hafta 7: Pumping Lemma for Regular Languages
Hafta 10: Pushdown Automata, Context-Free Languages and Pumping Lemma
Hafta 11: Pushdown Automata, Context-Free Languages and Pumping Lemma
Hafta 12: Turing Machines and Their Variants
Hafta 13: Turing Machines and Their Variants

courses.name: Operations Research

learning_outcomes: Define decision problems that may be encountered in practice
Formulate the mathematical model of the problem
Use appropriate solution approaches to solve the model
Analyze and interpret model solutions for optimal decisions
Use optimization software packages when necessary

Hafta 2: Development of linear programming models

Hafta 13: Transportation model and its types

Hamdy A. TAHA, “Operations Research: An Introduction”, 8th edition, Pearson Prentice-Hall, 2007.

courses.name: Summer Practice II
courses.purpose: To gain practical and technological experience in managing and carrying out real-world problems in industrial engineering and information organizations through individual and team work. To develop the ability to effectively turn the stages of implementing a defined problem into a report.
courses.description: Students do a 6-week internship in companies related to computer engineering. At the end of the internship, each student submits a technical report about their work.
learning_outcomes: Practical and technological experience in managing and carrying out real-world problems in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Experience in developing a system or application in industrial engineering and information organizations through individual and team work.
Ability to effectively perform software requirements analysis of a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations individually and within a team.
Ability to effectively perform software design of a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations individually and within a team.
Ability to effectively perform software development and testing of a defined problem in industrial engineering and information organizations individually and within a team.
Ability to effectively turn the implementation stages of a defined problem into a report in industrial engineering and information organizations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Observe and learn about the processes of managing/carrying out problems in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Hafta 2: Learn about the technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations, and receive the necessary training.
Hafta 3: Perform software requirements analysis and design of the technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Hafta 4: Perform software development and testing of the technical real-world problem to be solved in industrial engineering and information organizations.
Hafta 5: Turn the implemented system into a report.
resources: Before the summer internship: 1. Review the summer internship rules on the website. 2. Attend the summer internship seminar and ask questions. 3. Inform the student office and the internship committee about your internship start date 3 weeks before the actual start date.

courses.name: Computer Networks
courses.purpose: To teach the fundamental principles, protocols, and architectures of computer networks and to provide students with the ability to develop basic network applications.
courses.description: Introduction to computer networks and the Internet: access networks, network core, packet switching, circuit switching, protocol layers, and the history of the Internet. Application layer: Web, HTTP, FTP, e-mail systems, DNS, P2P applications, socket programming. Transport layer: UDP, TCP, reliable data transfer, congestion control. Network layer: control plane, data plane, datagram networks, IP, routing algorithms, broadcast and multicast algorithms, software-defined networking. Data link layer: error detection and correction techniques, multiple access protocols, Ethernet, link-layer addressing and switching. Current issues and problems related to computer networks, and the effects of computer networks on the environment, health, and security.
learning_outcomes: To be able to identify and analyze the causes of packet delay and loss in packet-switched networks.
To be able to design and develop network applications using socket programming.

To be able to explain the general structure of the Internet, basic network application protocols, and the fundamental principles of transport layer protocols such as reliable data transfer, congestion control, and flow control.
To understand and evaluate multiple access protocols and various error detection methods.
To be able to explain the services, basic protocols, problems, and solutions in the network and data link layers.
To be able to follow current issues related to computer networks and be aware of the effects of network technologies on the environment, health, and security.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to computer networks and the Internet I: access networks, network core, packet switching, circuit switching
Hafta 2: Introduction to computer networks and the Internet II: packet loss and delay, protocol layers, history of the Internet
Hafta 3: Application layer I: Web, HTTP, FTP, e-mail systems
Hafta 4: Application layer II: P2P applications, socket programming
Hafta 5: Transport layer I: UDP, reliable data transfer
Hafta 7: Transport layer III: congestion control
Hafta 9: Network layer I: virtual circuit networks, datagram networks, routers
Hafta 10: Network layer II: IP, ICMP, DHCP, NAT
Hafta 11: Network layer III: routing algorithms, broadcast and multicast algorithms
Hafta 12: Data link layer I: error detection and correction techniques, multiple access protocols, Ethernet
Hafta 13: Data link layer II: ARP, PPP, link-layer addressing and switching
Hafta 14: Wireless and mobile networks: CDMA, Wireless LAN, cellular Internet access, Mobile IP
Hafta 15: Network security: cryptography, message integrity, e-mail security, SSL
resources: J.F. Kurose and K.W. Ross, Computer Networking: A Top Down Approach, 5th edition, Addison Wesley, 2010.
A.S. Tanenbaum and D.J. Wetherall, Computer Networks, 5th edition, Prentice-Hall, 2010.
William Stallings, Computer Networking with Internet Protocols and Technologies, ISBN: 0-13-141098-9

courses.name: Introduction to Signals and Systems
courses.purpose: Modern sinyaller ve sistemler teorisini öğretmek.
courses.description: Ayrık ve sürekli zaman sinyalleri, dirac delta fonksiyonları, sürekli ve ayrışık sistemler, lineer sistemler, zamanla değişmeyen sistemler, tepki cevabı, konvolusyon, sürekli zamanlı Fourier serileri ve transformları, ayrık zamanlı Fourier serileri ve transformları, transfer fonksiyonları, Laplace ve Z- dönüşümleri, filtreleme, örnekleme teorisi, Nyquist örnekleme limiti, modülasyon.
learning_outcomes: Kompleks sayılar, dirac delta fonksiyonu gibi temel matematiksel kavramları öğrenme.
Sinyal, sistem, sürekli vs. ayrık, doğrusallık, zamanla değişmeyen ve nedensellik kavramlarını bilme.
Ayrık ve sürekli konvolüsyonu bilme. Konvolüsyonun LTI diferansiyel / fark denklemlerine nasıl uygulandığını öğrenme.
Fourier serileri ve dönüşümleri hakkında bilgi sahibi olma ve bunların konvolüsyon ile ilişkisini öğrenme.
Örnekleme, filtreleme, modülasyon, demodülasyon gibi uygulamaları öğrenme.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Komplex sayılara giriş. Euler kuralı.
Hafta 2: Sürekli sinyaller trigonometrik, üstel, indikatör, unit step sinyalleri.
Hafta 3: Sürekli sinc fonksiyonu ve dirac deltalar.
Hafta 4: Ayrık sinyaller, ayrık trigonometrik sinyallerde periyodiklik şartları.
Hafta 5: Ayrık sistemler, lineerlik, zamandan bağımsızlık, ayrık konvolüsyon, ayrık tepki cevabı.
Hafta 6: Sürekli sistemler, lineerlik, zamandan bağımsızlık, sürekli konvolüsyon, sürekli tepki cevabı.
Hafta 7: Fark ve diferansiyel denklemleri tarafından temsil edilen LTI sistemleri.
Hafta 10: Ayrık-zamanlı Fourier dönüşümleri. Ayrık periyodik sinyallerin ayrık zamanlı Fourier dönüşümleri.
Hafta 11: Ayrık-zamanlı Fourier dönüşümlerinin özellikleri. Frekans cevabı Konvolusyon özellikleri modülasyon özellikleri LTI sistemlerin frekans cevapları
Hafta 12: Sürekli-zamanlı Fourier serileri, dönüşümleri ve özellikleri. Frekans cevabı.
Hafta 14: Filtreleme, Örnekleme teorisi, Nyquist Örnekleme teorisi
resources: Signals and Systems, Oppenheim, Willsky, and Young, Prentice-Hall, 1983, ISBN No: 0-13-809731-3.

courses.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
courses.purpose: To equip students with the ability to explain and apply fundamental methods in supervised and unsupervised learning.
courses.description: Learning components, perceptron, linear models, error and noise, SVM, decision trees, artificial neural networks, dimensionality reduction, KNN.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental concepts of machine learning.
Apply basic supervised learning methods (KNN, decision trees, SVM, artificial neural networks).
Explain basic regression techniques.
Explain basic unsupervised learning techniques.
Apply machine learning techniques to real-world problems.

Pattern Classification, 2nd Edition, R. O. Duda, P. E. Heart, D. G. Stork, Wiley, 2000.

courses.name: Principles of Embedded Systems Design
courses.purpose: This course explains the fundamental concepts underlying embedded system design and helps students learn the general principles of embedded system hardware and software. It focuses in detail on the software and hardware components of the widely used ARM Cortex-M microprocessors.
courses.description: An overview of the ARM Cortex-M microprocessor family, ARM Instruction Set Architecture (arithmetic operations, load/store, flow control, subroutines), writing code by mixing C and ARM Assembly languages, memory map, interrupts, polling, general purpose input/output (GPIO), floating point unit (FPU), timers, pulse width modulation (PWM), analog-to-digital converter (ADC), digital-to-analog converter (DAC), and serial communication protocols.
learning_outcomes: Have knowledge about the basic components, structure, and working principles of an embedded system
Have knowledge about ARM microprocessor architecture and instructions
Understand programs written in ARM Assembly language, design and develop new programs, debug written programs, and design programs by mixing ARM Assembly and C languages
Have knowledge about input-output (GPIO), interrupts, timers/counters, and serial communication protocols for ARM Cortex M4 microprocessors, and be able to design programs using them
Be able to develop, test, and simulate code using both hardware and software components with the ARM-Keil MDK simulator
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Embedded System Design
Hafta 3: ARM Arithmetic Operations, ARM Load/Store
Hafta 6: Writing code by mixing C and ARM Assembly languages
Hafta 8: Interrupts, Polling and System Counter
Hafta 9: Memory Map and General Purpose Input/Output (GPIO)
Hafta 10: Memory Map and General Purpose Input/Output (GPIO)
resources: Yifeng Zhu: Embedded Systems with ARM Cortex-M Microcontrollers in Assembly Language and C, Publisher: E-Man Press LLC; 3rd edition (2017)

courses.name: İş Sağlığı ve Güvenliği II
courses.purpose: Çalışma ortamında bulunan fiziksel, kimyasal, biyolojik ve psikososyal risk etmenlerini ve bunlara karşı alınacak önlemleri, bu konudaki mevzuatı da inceleyerek öğrenmek.

learning_outcomes: Öğrenciler iş güvenliğinde tehlike ve risk kavramlarını öğrenmek ve iş kazalarından korunma yöntemlerini öğrenebilirler.
Fiziksel risk etmenlerini ve alınması gereken önlemleri tanımlayabilirler.

courses.name: Entrepreneurship and Innovation
courses.purpose: The overall aim of the course is to create within you the idea that someone has to concentrate on wealth creation and job creation within the context of market economies. You will be guided with a tool that is called “Quicklook,” which is designed to analyze a new technologies market potential. You will have an opportunity to create your “BIG IDEA” with your classmates as team members. The final project are for the teams to present their “BIG IDEA” to the class.
courses.description: Entrepreneurship and innovation are the principal source of jobs and wealth in market economies. This course is concern with entrepreneurship, with a special emphasis on technology transfer and wealth creation. Technology transfer is the process of taking innovations out of laboratories and finding commercial applications for those technologies. Although we will look at all kinds of entrepreneurship, the focus of the group project is on technology transfer and new venture development. The course is also concern with explaining “how” entrepreneurship takes place as well as “why” it takes place. The “how” of new venture development is related to the entrepreneurial process (innovation, technology transfer assessment, business plans, fund raising, launching of the enterprise, and the harvest or selling of the enterprise)?
learning_outcomes: To present a general understanding of how entrepreneurship and innovation fits into the economy;
to understand the importance of entrepreneurship to market economies and to understand how the field of Economics tries to make sense out of the economy
To get a complete overview of the entrepreneurial process so that we can introduce elements of that process as the class progresses.
To understand how why some regions are very entrepreneurial and others are not
To understand how entrepreneurship can be related to the experiences of newcomers to market economies
resources: Richard C. Dorf and Thomas H. Byers, Technology Ventures: From Idea to Enterprise
Alexander Osterwalder and Yves Pigneur, Business Model Generation
Gary Hoover, The Art of Enterprise and can be downloaded for $10 from the link below http://www.scribd.com/doc/25085990/The-Art-of-Enterprise-by-Gary-Hoover-January-2010

courses.name: Strategic Entrepreneurship
courses.purpose: Students will develop skills for evaluating, articulating, refining, and pitching a new product or service offering, either as a start-up business or a new initiative within an existing firm. This course is appropriate for all students interested in innovation and design as necessary components of new businesses today.
courses.description: Creating new businesses, capturing new markets, and enhancing organizational effectiveness occur through improving productivity or innovation, or both. New discoveries, new technologies, competition, and globalization compel both entrepreneurs and existing firms to foster innovation and agility. This course examines the theory and practice of promoting and managing innovation in start-ups and existing firms. It explores successful frameworks, strategies, funding techniques, business models, risks, and barriers for introducing break-through products and services. Topics include business model innovation, design-driven innovation, leadership, strategy, information technology, knowledge management, process improvement, performance measurement, and change management.
learning_outcomes: describe and analyse the nature of the macro and micro environments of importance to entrepreneurial activity
give explanations for driving forces behind dynamism and change in today’s business contexts in relation to practical entrepreneurship.
outline the process of entrepreneurship and analyse the context in which entrepreneurial opportunities may be identified in the environment
provide oral and written presentations at a master’s level
produce academic work independently, and through teamwork
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course overview, objectives and requirements. Introductions. Personal objectives.
Hafta 6: Creating and selling differentiated products/services
resources: Mauborgne, René, Blue Ocean Strategy, Boston, Harvard Business School Press, 2005. ISBN: 1-59139-619-0.
Snyder, Duarte, Unleashing Innovation, How Whirlpool Transformed an Industry, Jossey-Bass, 2008. ISBN: 978-0-470-19240-5.
Heath, Health, Made to Stick, New York, Random House, 2007, 2008. ISBN: 978-1-4000-6428-1.

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety I
courses.purpose: To provide students with general knowledge about occupational health and safety and to make them aware of the legal regulations in this field.
courses.description: The course is based on gaining knowledge about evaluating workplace conditions, understanding occupational accidents and occupational diseases, recognizing the importance of occupational safety and preventive measures, and becoming familiar with occupational safety legislation.
learning_outcomes: Students learn the basic topics they will need in occupational health and safety.
They can explain the basic concepts and historical development of occupational health and safety.
They gain knowledge about contemporary occupational health and safety application principles.
They gain knowledge about work safety in training laboratories.
They gain knowledge about adult education and adult education programs.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Development of the concepts and rules of occupational health and safety, general overview of occupational health and safety and safety culture
Hafta 2: Occupational health and safety in Turkey and in the world, occupational health and safety services

Hafta 11: Occupational health and safety in lifting equipment, occupational health and safety in hand tools
Hafta 12: Occupational health and safety in manual lifting and carrying operations, occupational health and safety in the design, manufacturing and use of work equipment

courses.name: International Business
courses.purpose: This course enables students to develop strategic perspectives in today's interconnected world and to understand and work effectively with conceptual tools by linking them to the changing environment, global industry, firm capabilities, and position.
courses.description: Companies today face an increasing range of options regarding markets, key activities for location, outsourcing, ownership models, organization, and cross-border management of processes. The course provides a framework for identifying and leveraging country- and industry-level opportunities in a dynamic global environment. It also focuses on strategic options at the firm level for entering activities, and addresses the challenges created by multiple perspectives, functions, and the interests of multinational firms.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes the importance of international supply and marketing chains.
Becomes aware of organizational challenges and their relation to outsourcing.
Gains knowledge about outsourcing in Turkey.
Knows the effects of operating business units in different locations on cost, resources, and marketing advantages.
Knows the advantages of 'home base' elements for firms.
resources: Ghemawat, Pankaj. Redefining Global Strategy: Crossing Borders in a World Where Differences Still Matter. Boston, MA: Harvard Business School Press, 2007
Marian Beise, "Lead markets: country-specific drivers of innovations", Research Policy, 33 (2004) 997-1018
Kasra Ferdows, "Making the Most of Foreign Factories" (1997). Harvard Business Review, March-April.

courses.name: Our Biosphere
courses.purpose: This course aims to help engineering students understand the biosphere and the changes caused by physical, chemical, and biological events, as well as the negative conditions created by industrialization and technological development.
courses.description: Formation of the world, big bang, ecosphere, hydrosphere, lithosphere and atmosphere; introduction to ecology and its fundamentals; basic components of ecology; beginning of life; pollution and its basic concepts; water pollution and its sources; air pollution, air pollutants and sources; soil pollution and soil formation; energy and environment; climate; greenhouse effect and ozone depletion; metal pollution and uptake of metals such as Hg, Cd, Cu, Pb, and Fe; homework presentations; environmental effects of metal industries.
learning_outcomes: Has knowledge about the formation of the universe and the world.
Understands the relationship between the beginning of life, its components, and the environment.
Is aware of natural and human-induced threats affecting the biosphere.
Has ideas about transferring a healthy biosphere to future generations.

Hafta 2: Introduction to ecology, fundamentals of ecology, and overview, Introduction to ecology, fundamentals of ecology, and overview,
Hafta 5: Pollution, basic concept of pollution, introduction to pollution,
Hafta 6: Receiving water pollution and sources,
Hafta 7: Air pollution, air pollutants and sources,
Hafta 9: Soil pollution and soil formation & Water pollution, basic pollutants and sources,
Hafta 12: Metal pollution, uptake of metals,Hg, Cd, Cu, Pb, and Fe etc.
Hafta 14: Environmental effect of metal industries to receiving,environment

Introduction to environmental engineering and science

courses.name: Self Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand themselves, recognize their goals, desires, perspective, obstacles, and unique voice, and gain knowledge about leadership.
courses.description: The course focuses on learning to lead oneself before leading others. It explores how effective leaders understand their strengths, areas for improvement, and how to manage people who may be better than them in certain aspects. The course is designed to help students discover their leadership tendencies, reflect on their life line and family history, identify what motivates them and what holds them back from achieving their goals, and develop the attitudes and behaviors needed to become a team leader. Topics include personal responsibility, brain capacity and improving focus, reflection on life and family history, learning how to learn, the power of affirmation, personal management, communication, strategic planning, stress management, and personal orientation.
learning_outcomes: Creates own motivation for change
Performs actions that make personal management successful
Develops personal leadership skills by identifying success traits
Learns to anticipate behaviors that will make oneself successful
Analyzes goals and develops personal objectives
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Brain capacity and improving focus
Hafta 3: Reflection on family history and life
Hafta 5: The power of positive affirmation

courses.name: Industrial and Economic Sociology
courses.purpose: The purpose of this course is to familiarize the students with basic topics of Industrial and Economic Sociology, a discipline from which interested Engineering students are expected to gain a lot of insight and knowledge.
courses.description: This course is an introduction to the sociological examination of economic phenomena. As a subfield that has grown rapidly over the past twenty years, economic sociology has focused on three major activities: First, it has examined the prerequisites for and constraints to economic processes as defined by economists. Second, it has extended economic models to social phenomena rarely considered in the domain of economics. Third, and most ambitiously, it has tried to search for alternative accounts of phenomena typically formulated only in economic terms. This course will provide an overview of these broad concerns and approaches in economic sociology, and review the sociological explanations of economic activities of production, consumption and distribution in a wide range of settings.
learning_outcomes: Enumerate the historical stages leading to capitalism, eventually.
Discuss mercantilism.
What do you visualize when one talks about “enclaves”?
Differentiate among Social, Cultural and Finally Human Capital.
What is globalization, in broad terms?

courses.name: Effects of Human Behaviour on Environment
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to examine the effects of human behavior on environment.
courses.description: Central to the design of this course are the notions that development occurs throughout the life cycle, from infancy through old age; observable behavior reflects both organismic (internal) and environmental (external to the individual) influences and their interaction; both person and environment comprise multiple, interacting levels and facets; understanding behavior requires a knowledge of universal principles, a respect for individual differences, and an appreciation of the dynamic tension between the two; oppression and struggling against it are rooted in both person and environment. Understanding human behavior and the social environment is central to achieving the social justice that is a primary goal of the field of social work.
learning_outcomes: Identify observable behavior and internal capacities associated with "normative," healthy, and problematic development across the life course.
Identify the wide range of social systems in which people live.
Identify the ways social systems and individual people promote / obstruct the achievement and maintenance of health, strength, and well-being in one another.
Apply theories and ideas from the liberal arts and physical sciences to understanding biological, social, cultural, psychological, and spiritual development.
Utilize conceptual frameworks / theories to guide practice processes of assessment, intervention, and evaluation

courses.name: Industrial Psychology

courses.description: Research methods in industrial/organizational psychology; decision-making standards; personnel decisions; organizational learning; performance management; organizations and organizational change; teams and teamwork; organizational attitudes and behavior; occupational health; work motivation.
learning_outcomes: Lists the related disciplines of industrial psychology.
Discusses F. Taylor's Scientific Management Theory.
Knows what to look for when discussing bureaucracy.
Can talk about the Human Relations movement in this field.
Knows how employee motivation can be increased further.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Industrial Psychology; first actual realizations of related ideas in Turkey in 1950s.
Hafta 2: What is Work and Why Do We Ever Work? (An approach according to the sociology of work).
Hafta 3: Work Organizations and Bureaucracy as an Early Model as proposed by Max Weber.

Hafta 4: Early Organization Studies@ Taylor’s Scientific Management Theory and Classical Organization Theory by Fayol at all. Structure and time-motion relations are considered. As for the human element, only economical motivation is yet recognized and thus payment for piece-wise task accomplishment is introduced.

Hafta 5: A Serendipity Example@ Hawthorne Studies at an electrical company near Chicago and the Discovery of Human Factors. Elton Mayo’s team is surprised with the results. The new conditions prepared by the experimenters creates a favorable atmosphere for the female workers and this sheer fact is also interfering with the investigation as it came to be known as the Hawthorne effect. This is a minor problem and no study is ideal, anyhow. The peculiar discovery in further stages shows that team solidarit
Hafta 6: Human Relations Movement, McGregor’s Approach and Maslow’s Famous Hierarchical Model of Needs (the Pyramid).
Hafta 7: Other Motivation Theories and Job Satisfaction. Two other motivation theories roughly approximating Maslow’s model are ERG Theory and F. Herzberg’s Theory.
Hafta 9: Expectancy Theory of Victor Vroom and Equity Theory of Stacy Adams.
Hafta 10: Modern Understanding of Management. International Emulations and Considerations on Management (The American way, the Japanese way and the in-between Z- modeling).
Hafta 11: Better Communication in Work Place; elements of communication, directions of communication (upward, downward and sideway flow). Ways to eliminate misunderstandings are considered.
Hafta 12: The Individual and Group Dynamics in Organizations; conformity, solidarity, cliques. Conflict and Personal Adjustment in the Work Organization.

Hafta 13: Supervision and Various Theories of Leadership (The difference between a manager / head and the real leader is especially stressed. Again, a 100 % “example” of any individual model being rare in real life; most actual supervisors are recognized as combinations of leaders and managers in miscellaneous proportions.

Hafta 14: Personnel Selection and Techniques of Appraisal (odds and pros of certain performance evaluation methods); On-the-Job-Learning; Decision-Making in Organizations (Common methods like creativity-stimulating brain storming and Delphi-technique).
Hafta 15: Counterproductive Behavior in Organizations and appropriate Measures for Their Elimination; Organizational Change and Resistance to Change; Good Mannerism at the Work Place.
resources: ARGYLE, Michael (1989). The Social Psychology of Work, Penguin Books, London
DeLEON, Emmanuelle (1993). Industrial Psychology, Rex Book Store, Manila, Philippines.
DECENZO, David. A. and Robbins, Stephen P. (1999). Human Resource Management, sixth edition, John Wiley& Sons, Inc., New York and Toronto.

Hafta 2: What is Sociology? What is Environment? What is then, Environmental Sociology?

Hafta 3: Big Disasters as Lessons@ Union Carbide’s gas leakage in Bhopal, India (1984). Tanker disaster’s spoiling seawater in Alaska (1989). Hurricane Katrina’s breaking levees in New Orleans (2005).
Hafta 4: Early sociologists only favored exploitation of the nature.
Hafta 5: Urbanization and Industrialization as Menace Against Nature. Robert Ezra Park’s contribution.
Hafta 6: Ulrich Beck, a Giant in the Field and his Views.
Hafta 7: Alan Schnaiberg, a Pioneer of Environmental Sociology and his Treadmill of Production Metaphor as a Critique of the Situation.
Hafta 9: The Interdisciplinary Nature of the Pollution Problems Require Cooperation between Social Sciences and Physical Sciences.
Hafta 10: Human Ecology, Social Ecology, Cultural Ecology and even Cultural Psychology@ All are Cousin Disciplines.
Hafta 11: Social Construction of the Environment.
Hafta 12: The Far History of Environmentalism@ Medieval Arabic Scholars, and King Edward I of England.
Hafta 13: Environmentalism as a Growing Philosophy with many Adherents.
Hafta 14: Individual Contributions to the fight with pollution.
Hafta 15: The Media’s Role in Commitment to the Cause of a Healthy Environment.

Mc CARTHY, Deborah & KING, Leslie (Ed.) (2009). Environmental Sociology from Analysis to Action, Rowman & Lıttlefıeld Publıshers, Inc. Lanham • Boulder • Toronto
